behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is local lint logic over template text. MOCK-MISS: this behavior targets one checker’s detection logic, not cross-module handoff. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none required. Rejected higher level: behavior explicitly says "static prompt-template lint check," which disqualifies System/Agentic/Workflow needs.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the lint function with template strings containing verbatim/near-verbatim phase instructions from SKILL.md and assert rejection with precise error spans.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation and fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundaries (template + interpolation + fixture assembly). Unit mocks could miss leakage introduced during composition. Integration-brake: no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, no real model-output dependency, no multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real harness prompt assembly with production-like fixtures and assert the final prompt text does not contain forbidden SKILL.md phase instructions.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Needs wiring across invocation, skill-loader, and rule source selection. MOCK-MISS: unit tests on one component can pass while invocation still uses hard-coded rules. Integration-brake: local file loading/wiring is enough; behavior text does not require real model judgment quality or multi-step agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Execute invocation path with real plugin/skill file inputs and assert the effective behavioral checks derive from loaded skill context, with hard-coded prompt rules disabled or absent.

behaviors.description: The harness can run the same user-style prompt against an intentionally degraded or missing SKILL.md variant without changing test prompt text.
behaviors.justification: Core risk is harness configurability and context-source swap while prompt text remains constant; that is cross-module state/input wiring. Integration-brake: no required real HTTP/subprocess; model-output quality not yet asserted here; no multi-agentic sequence required.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness twice (baseline vs degraded/missing SKILL.md fixture) using identical prompt text and assert only skill-context input changes between runs.

behaviors.description: When the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails under the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior changing due to skill-context degradation; deterministic stubs can hide this tautology/regression failure. Rejected Workflow: behavior specifies one "same user-style prompt" comparison, not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: With live model calls, execute identical user prompt against baseline and degraded SKILL.md contexts; assert degraded variant fails structural behavior criteria while baseline passes.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is pipeline ordering between model-response capture and parser/extractor modules. A unit test on parser alone cannot detect missed pre-parse artifact capture. Integration-brake: real model not required (stub response sufficient), and no OS/network/subprocess-specific behavior is essential.
behaviors.test_description: Run harness with a controlled model-response stub and assert a raw-output artifact is written before parse/extract stage executes (e.g., via ordered hooks/timestamps).

behaviors.description: Assertions enforce structural behavior constraints (for example schema paths and role-specific field placement), not only keyword presence checks.
behaviors.justification: Need to verify assertion stack used by behavioral tests, not just isolated predicate logic. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of one validator can pass while harness still gates on keywords only. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about assertion mechanism itself, not real model-output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run behavioral assertion pipeline on crafted outputs where keywords are present but structure is wrong; assert failure occurs due to schema/role-path violations.

behaviors.description: Under live model execution, structural assertions catch regressions that a keyword-only check would miss.
behaviors.justification: Explicit "live model execution" plus regression detection depending on model-produced structure requires real LLM behavior; mocked outputs can miss genuine distribution/format drift. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes a single live execution check type, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live-model behavioral smoke tests with structural assertions enabled and demonstrate cases where keyword-only checks would pass but structural constraints fail.
